description:
You are a log-analyst who prefers to work with a headless browser instead of opening pages in a GUI.  
    A small static web page that contains yesterday’s login events has already been placed on disk at  

        /home/user/data/access-log.html  

    1. Use *only* command-line tools that can operate in headless mode (for example a lightweight HTTP server plus a text-only browser such as curl/wget/lynx).  
    2. Read the HTML, isolate every line whose last field is the literal string “status:FAIL”.  
    3. From each “FAIL” line extract just the user name (the substring that appears after “user:” and before the two blanks that precede “- status”).  
    4. Produce a short report file at  

        /home/user/analysis/fail_report.log  

       The report must follow **exactly** this five-line format (line breaks matter and will be auto-checked):  

            fail_count=<NUMBER_OF_FAILED_ATTEMPTS>
            users:
            <first_failed_user>
            <second_failed_user>
            … (one user per line, keep the original ordering that appears inside the HTML)  

       • The first line contains only the keyword “fail_count=”, immediately followed by the total number of failed logins (no spaces).  
       • The second line is literally “users:” (all lowercase, colon included).  
       • From the third line onward list each failed username on its own line, preserving the order of appearance in the source file.  
    5. Do **not** include any other content in *fail_report.log*—no trailing blank lines, headings, or decorations.  

    The entire investigation can be completed with roughly three to four terminal commands (e.g. start a tiny server, fetch with a headless client, parse/redirect output).  
    When you are done, display the resulting report on the console so it is visible in the session transcript.
truth:
login log 
2024-05-05 10:01:22 - LOGIN - user:alice  - status:SUCCESS
2024-05-05 10:02:47 - LOGIN - user:bob    - status:FAIL
2024-05-05 10:03:19 - LOGIN - user:dave   - status:SUCCESS
2024-05-05 10:05:12 - LOGIN - user:carol  - status:FAIL
2024-05-05 10:06:54 - LOGIN - user:erin   - status:SUCCESS
]]>
